name: ElmSyntaxParserLenient
comment:
Like [`Elm.Parser`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Parser)
but able to parse badly indented code and similar somewhat incorrect syntax,
similar to elm-format.

This is **not** a fault-tolerant parser!
So if you write something it can't recognize in a file,
the whole thing will fail.

Also, precise ranges of some parts in in the parsed result are not reliable.
Though they will still be correct when viewed relative to each other
and will tell you how many lines they span.
This means [`ElmSyntaxPrint`](ElmSyntaxPrint)
can pick this up and format it in a way compatible
with the compiler or [`Elm.Parser`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Parser).

Some additional lenient parsing:

  - `f | g` → `f |> g`

  - `a != b` or `a !== b` → `a /= b`

  - `a === b` → `a == b`

  - `a ** b` → `a ^ b`

  - `\a => b` or `\a. b` → `\a -> b`

  - merges consecutive `,` in record, list or explicit exposing

  - removes extra `,` before first record field, list element or expose

  - removes remove extra `|` before first variant declaration

  - corrects `port module` to `module` if no ports exist and the other way round

  - corrects exposing `(...)` → `(..)`

  - corrects expression record field name-value separators

    `{ name : value }` or `{ name value }`

    → `{ name = value }`

  - corrects type record field name-value separators

    `{ name = value }` or `{ name value }`

    → `{ name : value }`

  - expands expression record field punning

    `{ field }` → `{ field = field }`

  - corrects `->` to `=` in an expression declaration and let expression declaration

    `function parameters -> result`

    → `function parameters = result`

  - allows omitting the name before the type in an expression declaration or let expression declaration

        : Type
        function parameters =
            result

    →

        function : Type
        function parameters =
            result

  - allows matching everything before

        3 |> String.toInt case
            Nothing ->
                0

            Just n ->
                n

    →

        case 3 |> String.toInt of
            Nothing ->
                0

            Just n ->
                n

  - moves import statements anywhere at the top level to the import section

@docs Parser, run, module_

That's all you'll need most of the time.

Sometimes it's useful to parse only some part of the syntax,
to, say, display only an expression in an article
or reparse only the touched declarations on save.

@docs Comments, commentsToList
@docs expose, exposing_
@docs moduleHeader, import_, declarations, declaration
@docs type_, pattern, expression


### whitespace

@docs multiLineComment, singleLineComment, whitespaceAndComments


### low-level

@docs moduleName, nameLowercase, nameUppercase
@docs RopeFilled

unions.name: RopeFilled
unions.comment:
Constantly appending lists of comments when combining parse can get expensive,
so we summarize everything in this temporary structure
and only convert to a list when we're done.

Inspired by [miniBill/elm-rope](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/miniBill/elm-rope/latest/)

aliases.name: Comments
aliases.comment:
A bag of comment nodes.
Each comment string contains the `{-`, `-}` or `--`.

Access with [`commentsToList`](#commentsToList)

aliases.name: Parser
aliases.comment:
Can turn a String into syntax or Nothing.
See [`ElmSyntaxParserLenient.run`](#run)

(This is not related to [`elm/parser`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/elm/parser/latest/).
[Open an issue](https://github.com/lue-bird/elm-syntax-format/issues/new)
if you need a way to covert to that)

values.name: commentsToList
values.comment: Extract a list of comment nodes from parse result [`Comments`](#Comments)

values.name: declaration
values.type: ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Parser { comments : ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Comments, syntax : Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Declaration.Declaration }

values.name: declarations
values.comment:
[`Parser`](#Parser) for a list of [`Elm.Syntax.Declaration.Declaration`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Declaration#Declaration)s
and comments in between
values.type: ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Parser { comments : ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Comments, syntax : List.List (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Declaration.Declaration) }

values.name: expose
values.type: ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Parser { comments : ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Comments, syntax : Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Exposing.TopLevelExpose }

values.name: exposing_
values.comment:
[`Parser`](#Parser) for an [`Elm.Syntax.Exposing.Exposing`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Exposing#Exposing)
(the stuff after `exposing` in an import or module)

values.name: expression
values.type: ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Parser { comments : ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Comments, syntax : Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Expression.Expression }

values.name: import_
values.type: ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Parser { comments : ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Comments, syntax : Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Import.Import }

values.name: moduleHeader
values.comment:
[`Parser`](#Parser) for an [`Elm.Syntax.Module.Module`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Module#Module)
(confusingly, that's their name for only the `module X exposing (Y)` lines)
values.type: ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Parser { comments : ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Comments, syntax : Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Module.Module }

values.name: multiLineComment
values.comment:
[`Parser`](#Parser) for a `{-...-}` comment,
also verifying that it itself isn't a documentation comment

values.name: nameLowercase
values.comment:
[`Parser`](#Parser) for a name used for
record field names and unqualified function/value references

values.name: nameUppercase
values.comment:
[`Parser`](#Parser) for a name used for
type names, variant names, record type alias constructor function names and module names

values.name: pattern
values.type: ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Parser { comments : ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Comments, syntax : Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Pattern.Pattern }

values.name: run
values.comment:
Turn a given source String into `Just` the parsed syntax
or `Nothing` if any unrecognizable part is found.

values.name: type_
values.type: ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Parser { comments : ElmSyntaxParserLenient.Comments, syntax : Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.TypeAnnotation.TypeAnnotation }

values.name: whitespaceAndComments
values.comment:
[`Parser`](#Parser) for the space between syntax tokens
which can contain spaces, linebreaks, [`multiLineComment`](#multiLineComment)s
and [`singleLineComment`](#singleLineComment)s

name: ElmSyntaxPrint

values.name: comments
values.comment:
Print `--` or `{- -}` comments placed _within a declaration_.
For top-level comments: [`moduleLevelComments`](#moduleLevelComments)

values.name: declaration
values.type: { portDocumentationComment : Maybe.Maybe (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node String.String), comments : List.List (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node String.String) } -> Elm.Syntax.Declaration.Declaration -> ElmSyntaxPrint.Print

values.name: declarationPort
values.type: { documentationComment : Maybe.Maybe (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node String.String), comments : List.List (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node String.String) } -> Elm.Syntax.Signature.Signature -> ElmSyntaxPrint.Print

values.name: declarations
values.comment:
Print a list of [`Elm.Syntax.Declaration.Declaration`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Declaration#Declaration)s
and comments in between
values.type: { portDocumentationComments : List.List (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node String.String), comments : List.List (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node String.String), previousEnd : Elm.Syntax.Range.Location } -> List.List (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Declaration.Declaration) -> ElmSyntaxPrint.Print

values.name: importExposing
values.comment:
Print the stuff after `exposing` in an import.
For module header exposing: [`moduleExposing`](#moduleExposing)

values.name: moduleExposing
values.comment:
Print the stuff after `exposing` in a module header.
For import exposing: [`importExposing`](#importExposing)
values.type: { atDocsLines : List.List (List.List String.String), comments : List.List (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node String.String) } -> Elm.Syntax.Node.Node Elm.Syntax.Exposing.Exposing -> ElmSyntaxPrint.Print

values.name: moduleHeader
values.comment:
Print an [`Elm.Syntax.Module.Module`](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/stil4m/elm-syntax/latest/Elm-Syntax-Module#Module)
(confusingly, that's their name for only the `module X exposing (Y)` lines)
values.type: { atDocsLines : List.List (List.List String.String), comments : List.List (Elm.Syntax.Node.Node String.String) } -> Elm.Syntax.Module.Module -> ElmSyntaxPrint.Print

values.name: moduleLevelComments
values.comment:
Print `--` or `{- -}` comments placed outside of declarations at the top level.
For comments within a declaration: [`comments`](#comments)

values.name: qualifiedReference
values.comment: Print a name with its qualification (`[]` for no qualification)
values.type: { qualification : List.List String.String, unqualified : String.String } -> ElmSyntaxPrint.Print

values.name: toString
values.comment:
All other helpers in this module produce a [`Print`](#Print)
which you can in the end convert to a String with [`toString`](#toString)
